🚀 1. Project Bootstrap (Clean + Isolated)

    # create env
    conda create -n proj_env python=3.11
    conda activate proj_env

    # create project
    django-admin startproject config
    cd config

    # create app
    python manage.py startapp booking

👉 Use config/ as root (better than project name duplication)

📁 2. Production-Oriented Structure

Refactor immediately:

    config/
    ├── config/
    │   ├── settings/
    │   │   ├── base.py
    │   │   ├── dev.py
    │   │   └── prod.py
    │   ├── urls.py
    │   └── wsgi.py
    ├── booking/
    ├── manage.py
    ├── .env
    ├── requirements.txt


⚙️ 3. Split Settings (MANDATORY for production)

base.py

Common config only

dev.py

    from .base import *
    DEBUG = True

prod.py

    from .base import *
    DEBUG = False
    ALLOWED_HOSTS = ["your-domain.com"]


🔐 4. Environment Variables


Install:

    pip install python-dotenv

.env:

    SECRET_KEY=super-secret
    DEBUG=True

    DB_ENGINE=django.db.backends.postgresql
    DB_NAME=booking_db
    DB_USER=postgres
    DB_PASSWORD=postgres
    DB_HOST=localhost
    DB_PORT=5432


📦 5. Install Core Dependencies


    python -m pip install \
    django==4.2.29 \
    djangorestframework \
    psycopg2-binary \
    django-redis \
    django-rq \
    "django-constance[redis]" \
    python-dotenv \
    django-simple-history \
    django-debug-toolbar


🗄️ 6. Database Setup (PostgreSQL)


python manage.py migrate

Create superuser:

    python manage.py createsuperuser


🔧 7. Add Apps (Clean)


    INSTALLED_APPS = [
        ...
        "rest_framework",
        "booking",
        "constance",
        "django_rq",
    ]


⚡ 8. Redis Integration (Cache + RQ + Constance)

You already did this well. Keep:

    Cache → DB 1
    Sessions → DB 2
    Constance → DB 3
    RQ → DB 0

👉 This is production-grade separation


🔄 9. Migrations Workflow (Correct Usage)


# after model changes

    python manage.py makemigrations booking
    python manage.py migrate


🧪 10. Run Services (Multi-process setup)


    Terminal 1 – Django
    python manage.py runserver
    Terminal 2 – Redis
    redis-server
    Terminal 3 – RQ Worker
    python manage.py rqworker default emails webhooks


📡 11. API Development


Create:

    # serializers.py
    # views.py
    # urls.py

Use:

    CreateAPIView
    ListAPIView
    JWT auth (you already added)


🧱 12. Production Hardening


✅ Turn OFF debug

    DEBUG = False

✅ Allowed hosts

    ALLOWED_HOSTS = ["yourdomain.com"]

✅ Static files

    python manage.py collectstatic

✅ Use Gunicorn

    pip install gunicorn

Run:

    gunicorn config.wsgi:application


🐳 13. (Real Production) Use Docker


Typical stack:

    Django (Gunicorn)
    Redis
    PostgreSQL
    Nginx


📄 14. Freeze Dependencies


    pip freeze > requirements.txt


🔥 15. Golden Production Rules
❌ Don’ts

    Don’t use SQLite
    Don’t use DEBUG=True
    Don’t use base environment
    Don’t mix Redis DBs

✅ Do’s

    Use PostgreSQL
    Use environment variables
    Use isolated env (conda/venv/docker)
    Use background workers (RQ/Celery)

🧠 Full Lifecycle Summary

    conda create -n proj_env python=3.11
    conda activate proj_env

    django-admin startproject config
    cd config

    python manage.py startapp booking

    pip install -r requirements.txt

    python manage.py makemigrations
    python manage.py migrate

    python manage.py runserver
    python manage.py rqworker


🚀 What You’re Building (Reality Check)


With your current setup, you’re already close to:

    Scalable backend
    Async job processing (RQ)
    Config management (Constance)
    Redis-backed infra
    PostgreSQL with indexing
